Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly chronic kidney disease. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to analytical or clinical factors. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment of glycemic status and renal function.
